description: Phase 4 - Runtime Integration and Local Developer Experience: Add dev scripts, .env files, health endpoints for non-HTTP services, service discovery, seed data with demo users, and developer tooling scripts (doctor, env:check, db:reset, smoke)
steps: 1. Add root dev scripts to package.json: 'dev:core' (runs core infra services - quantmail, quantchat, quantai backends), 'dev:apps' (runs all app frontends via turbo), 'dev:all' (runs everything). These use turbo filters. Also add 'dev:infra' script that prints docker compose instructions.

2. Create root .env.local.example with all env vars needed across the ecosystem (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, S3_*, SMTP_*, MEILISEARCH_*, NATS_URL, KAFKA_BROKERS, JWT_SECRET, OPENAI_API_KEY etc). Add .env.example to each app that lacks one (quantai, quantchat, quantcalendar, quantdocs, quantdrive, quantedits, quantmax, quantmeet, quantneon, quantsync, quantube).

3. Add a health HTTP server to non-HTTP services that lack one. Create scripts/lib/health-server.ts as a shared minimal Fastify health server module. Add it to services/search-indexer, services/moderation-worker, services/cdc-relay by importing and starting it alongside main logic. The git-server and ci-runner already use Fastify so add /healthz /readyz routes there. Services with no package.json (ads-api, ai-api, chat-api, edits-api, identity, mail-api, max-api, neon-api, sync-api, tube-api, ws-gateway) are stubs - create minimal package.json and src/main.ts with health endpoint for each.

4. Create packages/service-discovery/package.json and src/index.ts - a simple service registry that exports a typed map of service names to host:port with env var overrides. Export getServiceUrl(name) helper. Add to workspace.

5. Enhance packages/database/src/seed.ts to create named demo users: personal@quant.dev (personal user), admin@quant.dev (team admin), creator@quant.dev (creator), advertiser@quant.dev (advertiser), moderator@quant.dev (moderator), developer@quant.dev (developer). Add demo data: 10 emails per user, 5 chat conversations, 3 documents, 5 files, 2 videos, 10 posts, 5 calendar events, 1 campaign, 10 notifications. Keep existing random seed logic but add deterministic demo users first.

6. Create scripts/doctor.ts - checks Node version >=22, pnpm version, docker available, docker compose services defined, .env file exists. Create scripts/env-check.ts - validates all required env vars are set. Create scripts/db-reset.ts - drops and recreates DB, runs migrations, runs seed. Create scripts/smoke.ts - starts backends, hits /healthz on each, reports pass/fail. Add corresponding root package.json scripts: 'doctor', 'env:check', 'db:reset', 'smoke'.
7. Add tests: test for service-discovery package (unit test getServiceUrl), test for health-server utility, and update existing test expectations if any break.
8. Run pnpm typecheck, pnpm build, pnpm test, pnpm lint to confirm all gates pass.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm dev:core, pnpm dev:apps, pnpm dev:all scripts exist in root package.json
Root .env.local.example exists with comprehensive var list
packages/service-discovery exports getServiceUrl() with typed registry
packages/database/src/seed.ts creates 6 named demo users with demo data
Root package.json has doctor, env:check, db:reset, smoke scripts
findings: All quality gates pass: 71 typecheck, 50 build, 74 test, 60 lint. Stub services needed --passWithNoTests flag since they have no test files. The .gitignore needed .env.local.example exception added. Commitlint requires lowercase subject.
